attributes.description: Draws the switch in a checked state.
attributes.name: checked

attributes.description:
By default, form controls are associated with the nearest containing `<form>` element. This attribute allows you
to place the form control outside of a form and associate it with the form that has this `id`. The form must be in
the same document or shadow root for this to work.
attributes.name: form

attributes.description: The switch's help text. If you need to display HTML, use the `help-text` slot instead.
attributes.name: help-text

attributes.description: The name of the switch, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.
attributes.name: name

attributes.description: Sets the switch to a readonly state.
attributes.name: readonly

attributes.description: Makes the switch a required field.
attributes.name: required

attributes.description: The current value of the switch, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.
attributes.name: value

cssParts.description: The control that houses the switch's thumb.
cssParts.name: control

events.description: Emitted when the control loses focus.
events.name: syn-blur

events.description: Emitted when the control's checked state changes.
events.name: syn-change

events.description: Emitted when the control gains focus.
events.name: syn-focus

events.description: Emitted when the control receives input.
events.name: syn-input

events.description: Emitted when the form control has been checked for validity and its constraints aren't satisfied.
events.name: syn-invalid

methods.description: Checks for validity but does not show a validation message. Returns `true` when valid and `false` when invalid.
methods.name: checkValidity

methods.description: Simulates a click on the switch.
methods.name: click

methods.description: Gets the associated form, if one exists.
methods.name: getForm

methods.description: Checks for validity and shows the browser's validation message if the control is invalid.
methods.name: reportValidity

methods.description: Sets a custom validation message. Pass an empty string to restore validity.
methods.name: setCustomValidity

properties.description: Draws the switch in a checked state.
properties.name: checked

properties.description: The default value of the form control. Primarily used for resetting the form control.
properties.name: defaultChecked

properties.description:
By default, form controls are associated with the nearest containing `<form>` element. This attribute allows you
to place the form control outside of a form and associate it with the form that has this `id`. The form must be in
the same document or shadow root for this to work.
properties.name: form

properties.description: The switch's help text. If you need to display HTML, use the `help-text` slot instead.
properties.name: helpText

properties.description: The name of the switch, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.
properties.name: name

properties.description: Sets the switch to a readonly state.
properties.name: readonly

properties.description: Makes the switch a required field.
properties.name: required

properties.description: The current value of the switch, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.
properties.name: value

slots.description: Text that describes how to use the switch. Alternatively, you can use the `help-text` attribute.
slots.name: help-text

summary: Switches allow the user to toggle an option on or off.
